name: Goods movement services (Common Transit Convention / transport service)
pageTitle: Goods movement services - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Transport
heading: Goods movement services
bodyCopy: If yes, you must provide the Movement Reference Number (MRN) that will have been generated by NCTS.

legacyTemplate.notes: The page renders two independent, individually-toggled question blocks: the CTC block is gated by `enableCtcQuestion` (route handler default true — goods_movement_services.js:75) and the GVMS block by `enableGvmsQuestion` (default false, flipped on only when gvmsHelper.shouldDisplayGvmsQuestionOnGvmsPage returns true — goods_movement_services.js:74,80-94). The CHED-D trace rendered the CTC block, and trace b3742f19 rendered the GVMS block, so for CHED-D both blocks are observed present. The CTC legend copy has an `isChedpp` variant (goodsMovementServices.html:29-34): CHED-PP shows 'Are you using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) to move goods, such as to a control point?'; every other type (CHED-D / CED included, `isChedpp=false` at goods_movement_services.js:69) shows 'Are you using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) to move goods between countries?' — matching the confirmed CHED-D render.
legacyTemplate.distinctFromAddNctsMrnPage: A SEPARATE page, add-ncts-mrn (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/addNctsMrn.html), reuses the same ctc-question control name but is a 2-radio render with labels 'Add MRN' (value YES, pre-checked) and 'MRN not needed - consignment no longer using CTC' (value NO). It is NOT this goods-movement-services page. The two-option variant seen in the page object corresponds to that page, not to a second rendering of this one — see the ctc-question field note.

structure.text: Are you using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) to move goods between countries?
If yes, you must provide the Movement Reference Number (MRN) that will have been generated by NCTS.
Yes – add MRN now / Yes – add MRN later / No
Movement Reference Number (MRN) — text input revealed when 'Yes – add MRN now' selected
Will the transport use the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)?

govukComponents.usage: Groups each radio question (CTC question and GVMS question) with a medium legend
CTC question (3 options, first reveals MRN input) and GVMS question (2 options)
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 82; trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 87

govukComponents.usage: Movement Reference Number (MRN) — revealed conditionally under 'Yes – add MRN now'
CTC question hint, and MRN input format hint
'What is the CTC?' and 'What is the GVMS?' expandable explanatory content
'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' submit buttons
'Cancel and return to hub' link, plus GOV.UK guidance links inside the Details components

Three lists inside the Details explanatory content: 1 bulleted (govuk-list--bullet) and 1 numbered (govuk-list--number) in the 'What is the CTC?' details, plus 1 numbered (govuk-list--number) in the 'What is the GVMS?' details
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 82; corroborated by legacy template goodsMovementServices.html:103-117 (CTC bullet+number lists) and :150-175 (GVMS number list)

fields.name: ctc-question
fields.label: Are you using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) to move goods between countries?
fields.hint: If yes, you must provide the Movement Reference Number (MRN) that will have been generated by NCTS.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 82 (values: YES / YES_ADD_LATER / NO); trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 87. Page object drives this control: GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:6-19. CHED-D create/accessibility flows only ever check 'No' (radioCommonTransitNo) — ched-d-workflows.ts:324, ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:156 — so the Yes branch is unexercised by CHED-D tests; the 3-option render is corroborated only by the trace, not by a CHED-D test drive.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — PartOne.java:662-667 carries @NotNull on `provideCtcMrn` under group ProvideCtcMrnValidation; the not.null message is ValidationMessages.properties:204 'Select if using the Common Transit Convention (CTC)'. Enum values confirmed by ProvideCtcMrnEnum.java:4-6 (YES_ADD_LATER, YES, NO). The route handler enables this question by default (enableCtcQuestion=true, goods_movement_services.js:75), and the handler always writes provideCtcMrn from the payload (goods_movement_services.js:118). Note the front-end field-level Joi schema treats ctc-question as OPTIONAL (goods_movement_services_validation.js:29 — `Joi.string().optional()`); mandatoriness is enforced server-side via the ProvideCtcMrnValidation group, not by this page's own Joi schema.
fields.notes: MANDATORINESS is a legacy policy the rebuild may revisit: provideCtcMrn is required only when the ProvideCtcMrnValidation group is active (i.e. when the CTC question is shown, which is the default for this page). LEGACY-RESOLVED the earlier 3-option-vs-2-option-variant question: the 3-option control (ids ctc-question-yes / ctc-question-yes-add-later / ctc-question-no) is THIS page (goodsMovementServices.html:40-88). The 2-option Yes/No variant in the page object maps to a DIFFERENT page — add-ncts-mrn (addNctsMrn.html:28-64), whose two radios are labelled 'Add MRN' (YES, pre-checked) and 'MRN not needed - consignment no longer using CTC' (NO). So CHED-D on this goods-movement-services page always renders the 3-option control; the page object's #ctc-question-yes / #ctc-question-no are simply two of those three ids. Page object also exposes exact-label locators for CHED-A contexts (GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:6-11).

fields.name: ncts-mrn
fields.label: Movement Reference Number (MRN)
fields.hint: It is 18 characters, starting with 2 numbers, a 2-letter country code then 14 letters and numbers. For example, 24GB123456789AB012.
fields.conditionalOn: ctc-question = 'Yes – add MRN now' (YES) — revealed via govuk-radios__conditional; REQUIRED once that branch is selected

validation.errorMessage: Enter a valid Movement Reference Number
validation.evidence: goods_movement_services_validation.js:11-24 — when ctc-question==='YES', ncts-mrn is Joi.string().required().length(18).regex(NCTS_MRN_REGEX); otherwise optional/allow-empty. Regex NCTS_MRN_REGEX = /^\d{2}[A-Za-z0-9]{16}$/ (regexes.js:11). Error copy NCTS_MRN_INVALID = 'Enter a valid Movement Reference Number' (validation/messages/en.js:84), fired for STRING_LENGTH, STRING_PATTERN and STRING_EMPTY. NOTE the trace hint copy describes an 18-char shape as '2 numbers, a 2-letter country code then 14 letters and numbers' but the actual regex only pins the first 2 chars as digits and allows any alphanumerics for the remaining 16 — the '2-letter country code' constraint is copy guidance, not enforced by the pattern.

fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 82 (input name=ncts-mrn id=ncts-mrn, hint ncts-mrn-hint). Legacy template goodsMovementServices.html:55-64 (govuk/textInput id/name ncts-mrn, width-two-thirds, exact hint copy). Page object: GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:21-23 (inputMrn = getByRole('textbox', { name: 'MRN' })).
fields.notes: The reveal + MRN fill is never exercised by any CHED-D test (the CHED-D flow always answers 'No'), but the same shared page object drives it for CHED-A and CHED-P: radioCommonTransitYesAddMrnNow.check() then inputMrn.fill(generateNctsMrn()) — ched-p-workflows.ts:616-617, ched-a-workflows.ts:404-405,1230-1231. The generated MRN format (utils/reference-utils.ts:5-9: YY + 'GB' + 9 digits + 'AB012', e.g. 24GB123456789AB012) matches the trace hint exactly and satisfies the legacy regex, confirming the 18-char NCTS/CTC MRN shape. For CHED-D this field's presence is confirmed by the trace render; its input handling is inferred from the sibling CHED workflows and its validation rules are now sourced from legacy (see validation).

fields.name: gvms-question
fields.label: Will the transport use the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)?
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 87 (values: Yes / No, ids gvms-question / gvms-question-2); trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 82. Legacy template goodsMovementServices.html:130-139 renders the GVMS radios from `gvmsQuestionOptions` (goods_movement_services.js:84-93 — [{value:'Yes',text:'Yes'},{value:'No',text:'No'}]). Page object: radioTransportUseYes/No (GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:38-43) and id-based radioSelectGVMSYes = #gvms-question / radioSelectGVMSNo = #gvms-question-no (:32-37). Config field: useTransportService (ched-d-workflows.ts:58-59, default false → 'No').
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — PartOne.java:650-657 carries @NotNull on `isGVMSRoute` under group NotificationGvmsRouteValidation; the not.null message is ValidationMessages.properties:163 'Select if using the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)'. The GVMS question block itself is only rendered when gvmsHelper.shouldDisplayGvmsQuestionOnGvmsPage(notification) is true (goods_movement_services.js:80-94; enableGvmsQuestion default false), so this field is conditionally present AND conditionally mandatory.
fields.notes: MANDATORINESS is a legacy policy the rebuild may revisit: isGVMSRoute is required only when the NotificationGvmsRouteValidation group is active, which pairs with the block being displayed (GVMS route eligibility). CHED-D create/accessibility flows only ever check 'No' (radioTransportUseNo) — ched-d-workflows.ts:331, ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:157. The 'Yes' branch is unexercised by CHED-D tests; both options are confirmed present by the trace render. The handler stores this as a Boolean via yesNoToBoolean (goods_movement_services.js:126-127); CVEDP defaults a blank to 'No' (goods_movement_services.js:102-108) but CHED-D/CED does not.

validationMessages.trigger: No CTC option selected (provideCtcMrn null) while the ProvideCtcMrnValidation group is active
validationMessages.message: Select if using the Common Transit Convention (CTC)
validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:204 (key ...partone.provideCtcMrn.not.null); annotated at PartOne.java:662-667 under group ProvideCtcMrnValidation. Not observed in any trace — no empty-submit error state was captured for CHED-D.

validationMessages.trigger: No GVMS option selected (isGVMSRoute null) while the NotificationGvmsRouteValidation group is active
validationMessages.message: Select if using the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)
validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:163 (key ...partone.isGVMSRoute.not.null); annotated at PartOne.java:650-657 under group NotificationGvmsRouteValidation. Not observed in any trace.

validationMessages.trigger: ctc-question = 'YES' and the MRN is empty, not 18 characters, or fails the NCTS pattern /^\d{2}[A-Za-z0-9]{16}$/
validationMessages.message: Enter a valid Movement Reference Number
validationMessages.evidence: validation/messages/en.js:84 (NCTS_MRN_INVALID); wired for STRING_EMPTY / STRING_LENGTH / STRING_PATTERN in goods_movement_services_validation.js:11-19. This is a page-level Joi validation on the front end (goods_movement_services.js:34-41 renders the error back into VIEW_TEMPLATE). Not observed in any trace — the QA MRN generator only ever produces conforming values, so the negative case is untested.

config.note: The QA workflow notification-config (ched-d-workflows.ts) drives this page via three optional flags, all defaulting to the 'No' path — confirming the page is filled but not that either answer is mandatory:

fields.drives: downstream billing pages only — NOT a control on this page (see openQuestions)

openQuestions: MANDATORINESS is legacy policy the rebuild may revisit. Legacy IPAFFS makes both radio questions conditionally mandatory: provideCtcMrn under ProvideCtcMrnValidation (PartOne.java:662-667) and isGVMSRoute under NotificationGvmsRouteValidation (PartOne.java:650-657), each gated on its question block being shown. No CHED-D trace exercised an empty-submit error state, so the mandatory-selection messages ('Select if using the Common Transit Convention (CTC)' / 'Select if using the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)') are legacy-sourced, not confirmed by render. Confirm the new build wants the same conditional-mandatory policy.

RESOLVED (partially): the earlier 'CUC branch triggers downstream billing pages' note refers to CUC = Common User Charge (not Customs Union Code), driven by the `isCuc` config flag (ched-d-workflows.ts:71-72). The billing pages (confirmBillingDetails / findAnAddress / billingSelectTheAddress / billingChangeContactDetails) appear AFTER the Review Notification page (ched-d-workflows.ts:355-362), not as a control on this goods-movement-services page. Confirmed there is no CUC/billing control on this page. Whether CUC eligibility is derived from the internal-market question (a separate page) or elsewhere is a gap for a human.

RESOLVED (legacy): the MRN validation rule is now sourced. When ctc-question=YES the MRN is required, must be exactly 18 chars and match /^\d{2}[A-Za-z0-9]{16}$/ (goods_movement_services_validation.js:11-19, regexes.js:11); the single error copy for empty/wrong-length/wrong-pattern is 'Enter a valid Movement Reference Number' (en.js:84). NOTE the hint copy claims a '2-letter country code' segment but the regex does not enforce letters there — only the leading 2 digits are pinned. The error message itself is still legacy (not observed in any trace); mine an error trace only if render-exact copy is required.

RESOLVED (legacy): the CTC 3-option vs 2-option puzzle. The 3-option control (add MRN now / add MRN later / No) IS this goods-movement-services page (goodsMovementServices.html:40-88). The 2-option Yes/No control in the page object belongs to a DIFFERENT page, add-ncts-mrn (addNctsMrn.html), whose labels are 'Add MRN' / 'MRN not needed - consignment no longer using CTC'. CHED-D on this page always renders the 3-option control. Confirm with a human only if the add-ncts-mrn page is in CHED-D scope for the rebuild.

The 'Yes' answer to the GVMS question and both non-'No' CTC answers are never exercised by any CHED-D test — the create, arrival-date, accessibility and CUC specs all answer 'No' to CTC and 'No' to GVMS. Any downstream behaviour triggered by a 'Yes' on either question (e.g. required MRN, GVMS-specific pages) is unobserved for CHED-D and is a gap.

The CTC legend copy has a CHED-PP-only variant ('...to move goods, such as to a control point?'); CHED-D/CED uses the '...between countries?' form (goodsMovementServices.html:29-34, isChedpp=false at goods_movement_services.js:69), matching the confirmed render. No action needed for CHED-D — recorded so a shared-component rebuild does not lose the type-specific wording.
